html:
[403]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 刚直 - Means 'upright and resolute,' describing a person's character.
  • 恶不平事 - Means 'hates unjust matters,' indicating a strong sense of justice.
  • 赤缚于树 - Literally 'bound to a tree naked,' describing a person tied to a tree without clothing.
  • 情状甚惨 - Means 'the situation is very pitiful,' emphasizing the severity of the condition.
  • 息女 - Means 'daughter,' specifically referring to a daughter born to a man.
  • 媵妾 - Means 'concubine' or 'secondary wife,' indicating a lower status than a primary wife.
  • 干汝甚事 - Means 'What business is it of yours?' expressing impatience or dismissal.
  • 从人 - Means 'followers' or 'servants,' referring to people accompanying the main character.
  • 梦神指示耳 - Means 'a dream in which a god showed the way,' indicating a divine revelation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 叶刚直 - Refers to a man named Ye, who is described as upright and resolute.
  • 宿显宦 - Refers to a prominent official named Su, indicating his high social status.
  • 好行凶德 - Means 'likes to do cruel deeds,' suggesting a reputation for brutality.
  • 弟倚兄势 - Means 'the younger brother relies on his elder brother's power,' indicating a family dynamic where the younger brother uses his elder brother's influence.
  • 狂悖尤甚 - Means 'even more rebellious and defiant,' emphasizing the younger brother's extreme behavior.
  • 虎伏峪 - A place name, likely a remote and dangerous location, suggesting a refuge or hiding place.

Proper Nouns

  • 叶 - The name of the main character, Ye.
  • 张自防 - The name of the man who is tied to the tree.
  • 宿 - The name of the prominent official, Su.
  • 伍氏 - The name of Ye's wife.
  • 虎伏峪 - The name of the place where Ye is advised to seek refuge.

[404]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 伍独居 - Indicates a solitary state, meaning 'Wu lives alone.'
  • 夜未央 - Refers to 'deep into the night' or 'midnight.'
  • 叩门声 - Refers to 'knocking sound,' indicating someone knocking on the door.
  • 绝代彼姝 - Describes 'a beauty beyond compare.'
  • 伫立以泣 - Indicates 'standing still and weeping,' showing the woman's emotional state.
  • 官人 - Refers to 'a man,' often used in classical Chinese to address a male figure.
  • 奉事夫人 - Means 'serve the lady,' indicating the woman's intention to serve.
  • 平地生波 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'to create trouble out of nothing.'
  • 官人夫妇之信物 - Refers to 'a token of the couple,' specifically the jade lion as a symbol of their relationship.
  • 修仙 - Means 'to practice Daoist cultivation,' indicating a spiritual pursuit.
  • 代嫁 - Means 'to stand in for a bride,' indicating a substitution in marriage.
  • 觅原妻 - Means 'to seek the original wife,' indicating the man's search for his first wife.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 伍 - A surname, likely referring to Wu Zixu, a famous historical figure known for his loyalty and wisdom.
  • 张氏 - A common surname, likely referring to Zhang, and '张自防' is a specific person mentioned in the text.
  • 太史 - Refers to a high-ranking official in ancient China, often associated with historical records and divination.
  • 玉狮 - A jade lion, often used as a symbol of protection and good fortune in Chinese culture.
  • 詹氏 - Likely the name of a woman, possibly the original wife of the man being addressed.

Proper Nouns

  • 伍 - A surname, likely referring to Wu Zixu.
  • 张氏 - A common surname, likely referring to Zhang.
  • 官人 - A term of address for a man, possibly the man being addressed.
  • 太史 - A high-ranking official in ancient China.
  • 玉狮 - A jade lion, a symbolic object.
  • 詹氏 - Likely the name of a woman, possibly the original wife of the man being addressed.

[405]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 逃難 - Literally 'fleeing from disaster,' it refers to escaping from a dangerous situation, often due to war or natural calamity.
  • 改名 - To change one’s name, often done to avoid detection or for other practical reasons.
  • 貨馬作資 - Selling a horse to raise funds, indicating a desperate measure taken during difficult times.
  • 尋覓 - To search or look for something carefully.
  • 恣意游賞 - To freely enjoy and appreciate the scenery.
  • 受其徒而館焉 - Taking over the teaching position after the previous teacher's death.
  • 避難 - To seek refuge or shelter from danger.
  • 望救星照臨 - To hope for divine intervention or rescue.
  • 資賣卜以給食 - Making a living by selling divination services.
  • 備嘗險阻 - Having experienced many difficulties and hardships.
  • 相之曰 - Looking at someone and saying, indicating a divination or prediction.
  • 宜嫁遠方人 - Should marry someone from afar, suggesting a favorable outcome in the divination.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 葉長青 - A proper name, likely a character in the story.
  • 詹長青 - Another proper name, used as a pseudonym.
  • 伏峪 - A specific location, possibly a real place or a fictional setting.
  • 塾師 - Private teachers were common in traditional Chinese society, especially in rural areas.
  • 道姑 - A female Taoist priestess, often associated with divination and spiritual guidance.
  • 深山 - Deep mountains, often associated with seclusion and safety.
  • 莊 - A village or small settlement, a common setting in classical Chinese literature.
  • 峭壁插天 - A vivid description of the landscape, typical of classical Chinese poetry and prose.
  • 適村中塾師病故 - The death of a private teacher is a significant event in a village, often leading to changes in the community.
  • 張女 - Zhang's daughter, another character in the story.
  • 景某 - Farmer Jing, a character in the story.

Proper Nouns

  • 葉長青 - A proper name, likely a character in the story.
  • 詹長青 - Another proper name, used as a pseudonym.
  • 伏峪 - A specific location, possibly a real place or a fictional setting.
  • 張女 - Zhang's daughter, another character in the story.
  • 景某 - Farmer Jing, a character in the story.
  • 道姑 - A female Taoist priestess, a character in the story.

[406]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 吾负田力穑 - A humble self-description meaning 'I have been a farmer all my life, tilling the land.'
  • 终身不到三十里外 - Means 'I have never traveled more than thirty miles from home.'
  • 外交无人 - Means 'I have no connections outside my village.'
  • 何由嫁于远 - Means 'how could I marry someone far away.'
  • 五日后必有远方人来 - Means 'five days later, there will certainly be a person from afar coming.'
  • 美如冠玉 - A metaphorical description meaning 'as beautiful as jade, like a crown jewel.'
  • 身服齐衰 - Means 'wearing mourning clothes,' but here it likely refers to a person dressed in black or formal attire.
  • 手执报君知 - Means 'holding a divination board,' which is used for fortune-telling.
  • 为人算命者 - Means 'a person who tells fortunes.'
  • 屈指以俟 - Means 'counting the days in anticipation.'
  • 及期 - Means 'when the time came.'
  • 适至庄 - Means 'just arrived at the village.'
  • 观其容貌服色及手所执卦板 - Means 'observing her appearance, clothing, and the divination board she held.'
  • 悉如道姑言 - Means 'exactly as the nun had said.'
  • 此景之漏言 - Means 'this was a slip of the tongue by Jing.'
  • 而女未及细审 - Means 'but the woman did not investigate further.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 道姑 - Refers to a nun or a woman who practices Daoism.
  • 齐衰 - A type of mourning attire worn during the mourning period in traditional Chinese culture.
  • 卦板 - A divination board used in fortune-telling, often associated with the *I Ching* (Book of Changes).
  • 算命 - Fortune-telling, a common practice in traditional Chinese culture.

Proper Nouns

  • 道姑 - The nun or Daoist woman.
  • 张女 - The woman named Zhang.

[407]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 景曰 - Formal way of quoting someone, meaning 'Jing said.'
  • 君善风鉴否 - Polite inquiry meaning 'Are you skilled in fortune-telling or divination?'
  • 知其大概 - Means 'to know the general idea' or 'to have a rough understanding.'
  • 饭后 - Literally 'after eating,' but in this context, it likely means 'after the meal' or 'postprandially.'
  • 引之相宅 - Means 'to lead someone to inspect a house' or 'to take someone to view a property.'
  • 故呼女出 - Means 'therefore, he called his daughter out' or 'he then brought his daughter out.'
  • 后役以家事 - Means 'and later entrusted her with household affairs.'
  • 此为公何人 - Polite question meaning 'Who is this for you?'
  • 答以小女 - Means 'he replied that it was his younger daughter.'
  • 睨之 - Means 'to glance at her' or 'to look at her sideways.'
  • 佳冶窈窕 - Describes a woman as 'beautiful, graceful, and elegant.'
  • 既美且艳 - Means 'both beautiful and charming.'
  • 心计曰 - Means 'he thought to himself' or 'he mentally calculated.'
  • 美哉 - Exclamation meaning 'How beautiful!'
  • 吾见犹爱 - Means 'Even I find her lovely.'
  • 况男子乎 - Rhetorical question meaning 'How much more so for men?'
  • 敝舍何如 - Polite inquiry meaning 'How about my humble abode?'
  • 巽门坎室 - Refers to the layout of the house, with the door facing the southeast (巽) and the main room being the entrance hall (坎).
  • 厨居震方东 - Means 'the kitchen is located in the east (震).'
  • 四宅之上吉宅也 - Means 'it is one of the four auspicious houses.'
  • 但厨偏北方 - Means 'but the kitchen is slightly northward.'
  • 半属于艮 - Means 'partially belongs to the northeast (艮).'
  • 艮土受巽 - Means 'the northeast (Gèn) receives the southeast (Xùn).'
  • 震木克艮 - Means 'the east (Zhèn) wood overcomes the northeast (Gèn).'
  • 为少男 - Means 'indicating a younger son.'
  • 主小儿不旺 - Means 'indicating that the younger children will not thrive.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 景 - The name of the person speaking, likely a male figure.
  • 风鉴 - Refers to fortune-telling or divination, a practice common in Chinese culture.
  • 巽门坎室 - Refers to the traditional Chinese feng shui layout of a house, where the door faces southeast (巽) and the main room is the entrance hall (坎).
  • 震方东 - Refers to the east direction, which is associated with the element of wood in feng shui.
  • 四宅之上吉宅也 - Refers to the concept of auspicious houses in feng shui, where certain directions and layouts are considered favorable.
  • 艮土受巽 - Refers to the feng shui principle where the northeast (Gèn) receives the southeast (Xùn).
  • 震木克艮 - Refers to the feng shui principle where the east (Zhèn) wood overcomes the northeast (Gèn).

Proper Nouns

  • 景 - The name of the person speaking.
  • 巽 - The southeast direction in feng shui.
  • 坎 - The entrance hall or main room in feng shui.
  • 震 - The east direction in feng shui.
  • 艮 - The northeast direction in feng shui.

[408]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 似此何如 - Rhetorical question meaning 'What do you think of this?'
  • 厨系三楹 - Refers to a kitchen with three bays (a bay is a section of a building)
  • 将门移开南间 - Means 'move the door of the kitchen to the south bay.'
  • 水木相生 - A concept from Chinese philosophy where water nourishes wood, symbolizing harmony and balance.
  • 绝无妨碍矣 - Means 'there will be no hindrance.'
  • 羁旅之人 - Refers to a traveler or someone away from home.
  • 违家千馀里 - Means 'away from home by thousands of miles.'
  • 奚可就婚于此 - A rhetorical question meaning 'how can one marry here?'
  • 重服在身 - Means 'in mourning attire' or 'observing a period of mourning.'
  • 大祥后方可议婚 - Means 'only after the completion of the major mourning period can marriage be discussed.'
  • 资斧将尽 - Means 'travel funds are nearly exhausted.'
  • 不如暂应之 - Means 'it might be better to agree temporarily.'
  • 俟访得心上人再为计议 - Means 'wait until finding the right person before making further plans.'
  • 遂止于景 - Means 'so she stayed with Jing.'
  • 暗访叶生 - Means 'secretly sought out Ye Sheng.'
  • 不复云游矣 - Means 'no longer wandered around.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 厨系三楹 - Refers to the layout of a traditional Chinese house, where the kitchen (厨) is divided into sections (楹).
  • 水木相生 - A concept from the *Book of Changes* (易经) and *I Ching* (周易), emphasizing the harmonious relationship between water and wood.
  • 重服在身 - Refers to the practice of wearing mourning clothes during a specific period after the death of a close relative.
  • 大祥 - The second year of mourning, which is a significant period in traditional Chinese funerary rites.
  • 资斧 - Refers to travel funds or money for expenses.

Proper Nouns

  • 景女 - Refers to a female character named Jing.
  • 叶生 - Refers to a male character named Ye Sheng.

[409]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 葉色動，不語 - Describes Ye's non-verbal reaction to the question; he remains silent.
  • 葉勃然變色 - Indicates a sudden and intense emotional reaction from Ye.
  • 女決其為葉 - The woman concludes that the person in question is Ye based on her observations.
  • 弟特為覓兄到此 - The younger brother specifically came to find his elder brother here.
  • 君為宿某所使耶 - A question about whether the person is acting on behalf of Susu.
  • 奉令正詹伍夫人之命來耳 - Indicates that the person came at the command of Lady Zhan Wu.
  • 君失言矣！一人奚有二姓 - A logical argument against the idea of having two surnames.
  • 二姓之故，君自知之，何為故問 - A rhetorical question suggesting that the person already knows the reason for the two surnames.
  • 出玉獅一枚示之 - Describes the act of showing the jade lion.
  • 葉見之大駭 - Ye was greatly startled when he saw the jade lion.
  • 家藏之物，何得落外人手 - Expresses surprise and concern about the family treasure ending up in the hands of an outsider.
  • 女復袖藏之 - The woman then put the jade lion back in her sleeve.
  • 暇時為君細述之 - A promise to provide more information later.
  • 飯後，葉邀女移玉 - Ye invited the woman to move the jade after the meal.
  • 女答以繼至 - The woman replied that she would come later.
  • 葉俟至日暮，親身邀之 - Ye waited until dusk and personally invited her.
  • 女請詰朝相見，葉弗聽，握腕促行 - The woman asked to meet the next day, but Ye did not agree and grabbed her wrist, urging her to hurry.
  • 女不得已從之去 - The woman had no choice but to follow him.
  • 至，以玉獅授葉，葉視之，果其家藏 - Upon arrival, the woman gave the jade lion to Ye, who looked at it and confirmed it was indeed a family treasure.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 景某 - A person named Jing, likely a commoner or scholar.
  • 葉 - A person named Ye, possibly a teacher or scholar.
  • 張氏 - A woman named Zhang, likely a companion or guest.
  • 宿某 - A person named Susu, possibly a figure of authority or influence.
  • 詹伍夫人 - Lady Zhan Wu, a prominent female figure, possibly a noblewoman or influential person.
  • 玉獅 - A jade lion, a valuable and symbolic object, often used as a family heirloom or treasure.

Proper Nouns

  • 景某 - Jing (a person's name).
  • 葉 - Ye (a person's name).
  • 張氏 - Zhang (a person's name).
  • 宿某 - Susu (a person's name).
  • 詹伍夫人 - Lady Zhan Wu (a person's name).
  • 玉獅 - Jade Lion (a specific object).

[410]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 询其由来 - A polite way of asking for the origin or reason behind something.
  • 笑而不言 - A common expression indicating that someone is smiling but not speaking.
  • 以意度之 - Means to infer or guess based on one's understanding.
  • 假难成真，真难成假 - A paradoxical statement suggesting the difficulty of distinguishing between truth and falsehood.
  • 芳容如是 - Describes someone's appearance as beautiful or charming.
  • 勿怪宿某求之不得而不释然 - Means 'do not blame [the person] for not being able to obtain what they desire and thus not being able to let go.'
  • 初会 - Means 'first meeting' or 'first encounter.'
  • 相戏 - Means to playfully tease or joke with someone.
  • 相狎 - Means to become intimate or familiar with someone.
  • 悲喜交集 - Describes a mix of sorrow and joy.
  • 仍事男装 - Means to continue wearing male clothing.
  • 伪为朋友 - Means to pretend to be friends.
  • 值景初度 - Means 'during the celebration of [someone's] initial birthday.'
  • 席设内庭 - Means 'the banquet was set up in the inner courtyard.'
  • 乘间告曰 - Means 'took the opportunity to say.'
  • 非不欲为婿 - Means 'not unwilling to be a son-in-law.'
  • 非为婿之人 - Means 'not the type to be a son-in-law.'
  • 逃难访叶巧遇之故 - Means 'the reason for escaping from trouble, visiting [the person], and meeting by chance.'
  • 自脱其履示以下体 - Means 'took off her shoes to show her lower body.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 叶姓邑中甚少 - Indicates that the surname '叶' is rare in the town.
  • 张自防之子 - Refers to the son of Zhang Zifang, a historical or literary figure.
  • 芳容 - A term of beauty, often used in classical Chinese literature to describe someone's appearance.
  • 宿某 - Refers to a person, likely a historical or literary figure.
  • 景初度 - Refers to the celebration of someone's initial birthday, likely a significant event in the story.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '询其由来，女笑而不言' is elliptical, and the subject '女' (the girl) is implied.
  • '假难成真，真难成假' is a paradoxical statement that requires careful interpretation.
  • '芳容如是，勿怪宿某求之不得而不释然' is a complex sentence with multiple clauses that need to be carefully parsed.
  • '历述往事，悲喜交集' describes a mix of emotions, which can be challenging to translate.
  • '女请仍事男装，伪为朋友，叶笑应之' is an elliptical sentence that requires careful interpretation to convey the intended meaning.
  • '值景初度，席设内庭，女乘间告曰' is a complex sentence with multiple clauses that need to be carefully parsed.
  • '非不欲为婿，实非为婿之人' is an elliptical sentence that requires careful interpretation to convey the intended meaning.
  • '历述逃难访叶巧遇之故，且自脱其履示以下体' is a complex sentence with multiple clauses that need to be carefully parsed.
  • '叶某系诸生，世家后，年正青春，永无归期，得婚于此为赘婿，两有裨益' is a complex sentence with multiple clauses that need to be carefully parsed.

Proper Nouns

  • 叶 - A surname.
  • 詹 - Another surname.
  • 张自防 - A historical or literary figure.
  • 宿某 - A person, likely a historical or literary figure.
  • 景 - A person, likely a historical or literary figure.

[411]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 如不河汉吾言 - Rhetorical expression meaning 'if you do not consider my words as high as the river Han,' used to emphasize the seriousness or importance of the speaker's words.
  • 愿上令爱而己次之 - Means 'I wish to marry your eldest daughter and then your second daughter,' expressing a desire for marriage.
  • 此所谓鹬蚌相持，渔人得利 - Chinese idiom meaning 'this is what is meant by the鹬蚌相持,渔人得利' (the鹬 and the蚌 are in conflict, and the fisherman benefits), used to describe a situation where two parties are in conflict, and a third party benefits.
  • 吾不惯看活春宫 - Colloquial expression meaning 'I am not used to seeing live action pornography,' used to express discomfort or disapproval of a situation.
  • 二年前尝有人见二女子携手入峪，未闻见其出者 - Narrative expression meaning 'two years ago, someone saw two women holding hands enter the valley, but no one has heard of them coming out,' used to describe a mysterious event.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 河汉 - Refers to the Milky Way, used here as a metaphor for something very high or far away.
  • 令爱 - A respectful term for 'your daughter.'
  • 鹬蚌相持，渔人得利 - A Chinese idiom that originates from a fable in the *Strategies of the Warring States* (《战国策》), where two animals are in conflict, and a third party benefits.
  • 春宫 - Refers to erotic paintings or images, often used in a derogatory sense.
  • 摩天岭 - Refers to a mountain range, possibly a specific location in the story.
  • 峪 - Refers to a valley or ravine, often associated with dangerous or mysterious places in Chinese folklore.

[412]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 盤桓多時 - Linger for a long time, often used to describe someone who stays in one place for an extended period, possibly out of indecision or reluctance.
  • 悒悒而歸 - Return home with a heavy heart, describing a state of deep dissatisfaction or melancholy.
  • 不知福地，無處逃生，命也 - Rhetorical statement indicating that not knowing about a safe haven and having no place to escape to is a matter of fate.
  • 既知其地，坐以待毙，非命也 - Rhetorical statement suggesting that knowing about a safe place but choosing to wait for death is not a matter of fate but rather a choice.
  • 九死一生 - Extremely dangerous situation where survival is extremely difficult.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 福地 - Blessed land or paradise, often used in classical Chinese literature to refer to a safe or ideal place.
  • 忠臣孝子 - Loyal ministers and filial sons, idealized figures in Confucian ethics.
  • 君父 - Ruler and father, emphasizing the hierarchical and familial relationships in traditional Chinese society.
  • 濟君父之難 - Help the ruler or father in times of difficulty, often used to describe acts of loyalty and filial piety.

Proper Nouns

  • 葉 - The name of the husband.
  • 張氏 - The name of the wife.
  • 詹氏 - The name of a person or family, possibly a previous owner of the object.

[413]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 只身入峪 - Entering the valley alone; emphasizes Zhang's solitude and isolation.
  • 遍兽蹄鸟迹 - Covered with animal tracks and bird prints; indicates a wilderness setting.
  • 径无人蹊 - There are no human paths; reinforces the wild and untamed nature of the environment.
  • 毛发猬竖 - Hair standing on end like a hedgehog; describes Zhang's fear and tension.
  • 手无寸铁 - Not carrying even a single weapon; highlights Zhang's vulnerability.
  • 举玉狮作遥击之势 - Raising the jade lion to make a remote attack gesture; describes Zhang's defensive action.
  • 不敢前 - Not daring to advance; indicates the tigers' fear of the jade lion.
  • 莫解所以 - Cannot understand why; expresses Zhang's confusion about the tigers' behavior.
  • 翠柏苍松 - Green cypress and dark pine trees; symbolizes longevity and resilience.
  • 瞑目如睡 - With closed eyes as if sleeping; describes the nun's meditative state.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 峪 - Refers to a valley; a common geographical feature in Chinese landscapes.
  • 虎啸猿吟 - Describes the sounds of tigers roaring and monkeys howling; typical of a wild and untamed environment.
  • 玉狮 - A jade lion; often used as a talisman or protective amulet in Chinese culture.
  • 洞口 - Refers to the entrance of a cave; often associated with mystical or sacred places in Chinese folklore.
  • 一道姑 - A Taoist nun; indicates a religious or spiritual setting.
  • 高坐 - Sitting upright; often associated with meditation or deep concentration.

Proper Nouns

  • 张氏 - Refers to Zhang, the protagonist of the story.
  • 玉狮 - A jade lion; a specific object in the story.
  • 一道姑 - A Taoist nun; a specific character in the story.

[414]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 见张，惊曰 - Direct quotation indicating surprise and reaction upon seeing someone.
  • 汝何得到此 - Question expressing disbelief about how someone arrived at a particular place.
  • 汝叶氏耶 - Question asking if someone is of the Leaf family or associated with it.
  • 非氏叶也 - Response clarifying that the person is not of the Leaf family but has the surname Leaf.
  • 何为来者 - Question asking why someone has come.
  • 遂将叶公子为不平伤人逃难，一一细言之 - Statement describing a detailed account of the reasons for coming, including an incident involving a nobleman named Ye Gong and fleeing from danger.
  • 忽悲忽喜 - Phrase indicating sudden changes in emotion, from sadness to joy.
  • 心知仙姑必詹氏 - Statement expressing internal knowledge or belief that the nun is definitely named Zhan.
  • 伍姊代嫁者非子耶 - Question asking if the person being referred to is the one who was substituted in marriage for the sister.
  • 妾等误入局中，欲出不得 - Statement indicating that the speaker and others were mistakenly caught in a situation and cannot escape.
  • 不如姊之托身局外得自由也 - Comparison suggesting that it is better to be outside the situation and enjoy freedom.
  • 吾身出局外，心在局中 - Statement expressing a paradoxical situation where the body is free but the mind is still involved in the situation.
  • 身心两地，苦尤异常 - Statement indicating that the separation of body and mind leads to unusual suffering.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 龍君 - A term used in Chinese mythology to denote a powerful dragon deity or ruler of the dragon realm. In Daoist and folk traditions, dragons are often associated with water, storms, and supernatural power.
  • 贰室 - A term that might indicate a secondary or subordinate dwelling within the dragon realm, possibly used for specific purposes such as celebrations or meetings.
  • 贺新郎 - A reference to a wedding or nuptial celebration. In classical Chinese, *新郎* (xīn láng) means 'new groom,' and the phrase implies that the setting is being used for a wedding or similar occasion.
  • 兰麝 - Refers to *lan* (orchid) and *she* (musk), both of which are fragrant substances used in traditional Chinese perfumery and incense, symbolizing luxury and refinement.
  • 下界鲰生 - *下界* (xià jiè) means 'lower realms' or 'the mortal world,' and *鲰生* (zōu shēng) is a modest and self-deprecating term for oneself, often used by scholars to express humility.
  • 钧天之奏 - *钧天* (jūn tiān) refers to the 'music of the heavens,' a term from Daoist and Confucian cosmology, associated with the music of the celestial realm, often used to describe divine or transcendent music.
  • 三生之幸 - *三生* (sān shēng) means 'three lifetimes,' a Buddhist concept referring to past, present, and future lives, or the three realms of existence. It is used here to emphasize the rarity and profundity of the experience.
  • 草束赤须龙 - A dragon with red whiskers bound with grass. This is a specific image of a dragon, possibly from a folk tale or legend, indicating a supernatural being of power.
  • 月仙 - *月仙* (yuè xiān) is a term for a 'moon fairy' or 'moon immortal,' a female supernatural being often associated with the moon in Chinese mythology and poetry.
  • 玉簪 - A *yù zān* is a jade hairpin, a traditional Chinese accessory worn by women. In this context, it serves as a magical object, used to summon a boat.
  • 名山大川 - *Míng shān dà chuān* means 'famous mountains and great rivers,' referring to the natural landscapes of China, often used metaphorically in literature.

  • 不几日，黄水忽到，举家登楼 - A few days later, the yellow floodwaters suddenly arrived, and the whole family climbed to the upper floor; '不几日' means 'a few days later,' and '黄水' refers to the floodwaters.
  • 眼看庄舍倾圮，已成水国，而水将及楼门，成大惧 - They saw their village and houses collapse, turning into a watery kingdom, and the water was about to reach the door of the upper floor, causing the son great fear; '倾圮' means 'collapse,' and '水国' means 'watery kingdom.'
  • 媪指处，漂来大木二段，如屋梁 - The maid pointed, and two large pieces of wood floated by, like roof beams; '漂来' means 'floated by,' and '如屋梁' means 'like roof beams.'
  • 成言与老母跨一木，所有银两载于其上；家人共跨一木 - The son said that he and his mother would cross on one piece of wood, with all their silver on it; the rest of the family would cross together on the other piece of wood; '载于其上' means 'carried on it.'
  • 而成实意一木不能载重，将摒弃银两以拯母，所以必言载银两者，恐家人分跨为母累也 - But in reality, one piece of wood could not bear the weight, so he intended to abandon the silver to save his mother. That is why he insisted on carrying the silver, fearing that if the family split the load, it would burden his mother; '实意' means 'in reality,' and '摒弃银两' means 'abandon the silver.'
  • 方跨木，媪已杳，楼亦淹没，而二木不浮不沉，虽湿下衣，而稳如舟车 - As they were crossing on the wood, the maid disappeared, and the upper floor was also submerged, but the two pieces of wood neither floated nor sank, although they got wet, they were as stable as a boat or a carriage; '杳' means 'disappeared,' and '稳如舟车' means 'as stable as a boat or a carriage.'

  • The sentence '惠曰：“试君耳。妾如见弃于彼，君亦不得到此。”' explains the purpose of the previous night's act, which is to test the relationship.
  • The sentence '吉曰：“妾如见弃于彼，君亦不得到此。”' reiterates the condition set by the previous night's act.
  • The sentence '惠曰：“原期吾子完婚日，使君受新妇参拜。以君恋恋之情，致君多享福数年。”' expresses a wish for the future, linking the current situation to a future event.
  • The sentence '吉曰：“君知演戏庆祝之深意乎？”' asks about the deeper meaning behind the celebration, which is a bit ambiguous.
  • The sentence '惠曰：“不知也。”' indicates that the speaker does not understand the deeper meaning.
  • The sentence '惠曰：“吾儿已长大，人以妾家无男老，无与为婚。”' explains why the son is being considered for marriage, due to the lack of male heirs in the family.
  • The sentence '人今见君父子翩翩，提亲者将接踵而至。”' predicts that many families will offer their daughters for marriage due to the positive impression of the family.
  • The sentence '惠择淑女有福相者，聘为妇。' describes the process of selecting a suitable wife for the son.
  • The sentence '惠忽欲归省，吉曰：“从未归省，今何顿异素行？”' introduces a sudden change in behavior, which is a bit ambiguous in terms of the exact reason.
  • The sentence '惠曰：“曩之归也，夜去明来，君自不知。”' explains the previous night's act, which was a secret visit.
  • The sentence '今去不即返，将住数日，以休妾心，不得不告。”' explains the current situation, where the speaker will stay for a few days to rest.
  • The sentence '嗣无耗。' indicates that there was no further news after the speaker left.

  • This phrase describes Wu's state of mind and actions, emphasizing her confusion and the desperate situation.
  • This phrase indicates the gradual reduction of companions, leaving only one young woman.
  • This phrase highlights the financial dependency of the woman on Wu.
  • This phrase shows Wu's growing suspicion and questioning of their journey.
  • This phrase reveals the woman's deceit and manipulation.
  • This phrase captures Wu's helplessness and submission to the woman's commands.
  • This phrase emphasizes the depletion of Wu's resources.
  • This phrase describes the dire situation and Wu's decision to follow the woman's advice.
  • This phrase introduces the historical figure and the reason for buying Wu.
  • This phrase concludes the story with the discovery of the woman's absence and Wu's denial of having a mother-in-law.

  • 當途拜父 - To bow to the father in the middle of the road. A gesture of respect and filial piety, performed in public.

  • 惟點額而已 - He could only nod his head. A gesture of approval or acknowledgment, used when one cannot speak.
  • 若非見血娃而動恻隐 - If not for seeing the child (blood-child) and being moved by compassion. This phrase implies that the speaker's action (Ri’s) was prompted by compassion.
  • 何得享此厚福？ - How could he then receive such great blessings? A rhetorical question expressing gratitude or surprise at being blessed.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sequence '忽自外来一大汉，连顾左右，立顾身后视之' must be read as a single, continuous description of the man's actions and appearance.
  • The question '神案之石子，其君投于殿隅耶' is a key point in the narrative, inquiring about the origin of the stone.
  • The man's request '有一事相商，祈君移玉从我去' is a pivotal moment, asking Con to follow him.
  • The man's aggressive action '握顾腕强之' is a turning point in the story.
  • The journey '山径崎岖，行甚不易，约五、六里，至一幽谷石室中' is described in a series of actions, emphasizing the difficulty of the path.
  • The man's boastful statement '吾请得大王来矣' is a key moment of tension, claiming he has brought the great king.
  • The group's reaction '众悉起，逊顾上坐' is a significant social interaction, showing respect for Con.
  • The group's plan and character are described in a series of actions: '作无本生意，悉粗率无谋，欲请文学之士，推作首领'.
  • The group's practices are explained in a series of actions: '庙中神案之石子，壁间之飞禽，皆假之为卜缘也'.
  • The mystical interpretation '君悉应之，可知天缘有分矣' is a key moment of tension, suggesting that fate has a plan.
  • Con's realization and refusal '知为伙贼，力辞之' are the final moments of the passage, revealing the group's true nature and his decision to reject them.

  • 昔车三翡翠玉玩实吾藏置鼠穴 - Means 'The three jade carvings of emerald green were actually hidden by me in a rat's hole.'

  • 汝知务施报 - Means 'you know the principle of repaying kindness.'

  • 君善卜 - Means 'you are good at divination.'
  • 请即为吾卜之 - Means 'please divine for me immediately.'
  • 一时 - Means 'this moment.'
  • 时为戌初 - Means 'the time is戌初 (戌初 is a specific time in the Chinese time system).'
  • 遂袖占之 - Means 'he then divined by holding something in his sleeve.'
  • 大祸在即 - Means 'a great disaster is imminent.'
  • 可急赴旅店避之 - Means 'you should hurry to a inn to avoid it.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 副贡 - Refers to a junior scholar-official, a lower-ranking official appointed through the imperial examination system.
  • 营生 - Refers to one's livelihood or occupation.
  • 不法 - Refers to illegal or unlawful activities.
  • 自寻苦恼 - A common expression meaning to cause oneself trouble.
  • 卜 - Refers to divination, a traditional Chinese practice for predicting the future.
  • 戌初 - A specific time in the Chinese time system, which divides the day into 12 two-hour periods.
  • 袖占 - A form of divination where one holds an object in their sleeve and interprets signs.
  • 旅店 - Refers to an inn or lodging place.

Means 'But when the angry thieves raised their knives, I was deeply afraid that Zhang Liu would come a little late'.
  • 自富民起程时，妾即欲别君 - 自 means 'since', 富民 means 'wealthy man', 起程 means 'set out', 时 means 'when', 妾 means 'I', 即 means 'immediately', 欲 means 'wanted to', 别君 means 'part from you'. Means 'Since the wealthy man set out, I immediately wanted to part from you'.

  • 归，于路忽腹痛难支 - Means 'on returning, suddenly on the road, he suffered from severe abdominal pain that he could hardly bear.' The phrase '难支' emphasizes the intensity of the pain.
  • 急走 - Means 'ran hastily' or 'ran quickly.'
  • 忽见一第 - Means 'suddenly saw a mansion.' The character '第' refers to a large house or mansion.

  • 意欲急前哀其烦人医治 - Means 'intending to go forward quickly and beg her to trouble herself to treat him.' The phrase '意欲' expresses intention, and '哀其烦人医治' is a polite request.
  • 尚违数武 - Means 'still several steps away.' The character '武' is an ancient unit of distance.
  • 倒地不能言 - Means 'fell to the ground unable to speak.' The phrase '不能言' indicates his inability to communicate.
  • 而心甚明了 - Means 'but his mind was very clear.' The phrase '明了' emphasizes his mental clarity despite physical distress.
  • 少妇急遣人扶命入客舍 - Means 'the young woman quickly sent someone to carry him into the guesthouse.' The phrase '急遣人' indicates urgency, and '扶命' means 'carry him.'
  • 卧榻上 - Means 'lay on the bed.' The phrase '卧榻上' specifies his position.
  • 此等暴病，立能毙人 - Means 'such sudden illnesses can kill a person instantly.' The phrase '立能毙人' emphasizes the immediacy of the danger.
  • 再迟片刻，即不治 - Means 'if delayed even a moment, it would be incurable.' The phrase '即不治' indicates the critical nature of the situation.
  • 急以药丸纳命口，以清水送下 - Means 'quickly gave him a pill to put in his mouth and had him swallow it with water.' The phrase '急以药丸纳命口' describes the action of giving the pill, and '以清水送下' describes the method of administration.
  • 少时，痛顿止，口亦能言 - Means 'after a short while, the pain suddenly stopped, and he could speak again.' The phrase '痛顿止' emphasizes the sudden relief, and '口亦能言' indicates his recovery of speech.
  • 但四肢无力不能起 - Means 'but his limbs were weak and he could not stand up.' The phrase '但' introduces a contrast.
  • 汝顾命耶 - Means 'Do you want to live?' The phrase '顾命' is a formal way of asking if someone wants to continue living.
  • 然 - A simple affirmative response meaning 'yes.'
  • 穷困如是，酒赌宜戒也 - Means 'In such poverty, gambling and drinking should be avoided.' The phrase '穷困如是' describes the dire circumstances, and '酒赌宜戒也' is a moral exhortation.
  • 如能戒之，吾语汝以生全之门 - Means 'If you can abstain, I will tell you the way to preserve your life.' The phrase '生全之门' refers to a means of survival or preservation.
  • 矢从此戒之 - Means 'I vow to abstain from now on.' The phrase '矢从此戒之' expresses a solemn promise.
  • 汝归，至邑东门内某店 - Means 'When you return, go to a certain shop inside the eastern gate of the county.' The phrase '至邑东门内某店' specifies the location.
  • 问有客名勾成者 - Means 'ask for a guest named Gou Cheng.' The phrase '问有客名勾成者' is a request for information.
  • 自道姓名，渠必厚恤也 - Means 'introduce yourself by name, and he will certainly show great kindness.' The phrase '自道姓名' means 'introduce yourself by name,' and '渠必厚恤也' indicates the expected response.
  • 言已，执灯掩扉去 - Means 'After speaking, she took the lamp and closed the door and left.' The phrase '执灯掩扉去' describes her actions after the conversation.

  • 若然，五日后再议 - Sets a future meeting time for further discussion; If so, let's discuss it again in five days.
  • 嗣郝果出资给命 - Describes the fulfillment of the agreement; Subsequently, Mr. Hao indeed provided the capital to Wang.
  • 命福至运转 - Suggests a change in fortune or circumstances; Wang's luck turned around.
  • 获利较勾加倍 - Compares the profits between Wang and Gou; The profit was double what Gou made.
  • 岁终，携利面郝清算 - Describes the process of settling accounts; At the end of the year, Wang brought the profits to Mr. Hao for settlement.
  • 盖本资实令堂所出 - Reveals the true source of the capital; Actually, the capital was provided by your mother.
  • 子连年所衣之棉衣，悉令堂亲身送到 - Highlights the mother's care and involvement; The cotton clothes you have worn for years were all personally delivered by your mother.
  • 且屡问子之棉衣尚衣之否 - Emphasizes the mother's concern and attention to detail; She also frequently asked if you were still wearing those cotton clothes.
  • 令堂为子操心洵苦矣 - Expresses the mother's deep concern and effort; Your mother has indeed been very troubled for you.
  • 儿虽乞丐为生，未受饥饿 - Reflects Wang's gratitude despite his humble circumstances; Although I have lived as a beggar, I have not suffered from hunger.
  • 吾见汝喜出望外，但忆汝历受百苦，为之伤心耳 - Conveys the mother's mixed emotions of joy and sorrow; I am delighted to see you, but I recall all the hardships you have endured, which makes me sad.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 乞丐为生 - Refers to living as a beggar, a common theme in Chinese literature reflecting the social and economic conditions of the time.
  • 衣之 - Refers to wearing clothes, a basic human need and a symbol of care and provision.
  • 令堂 - Refers to one's mother, emphasizing the respect and filial piety in Chinese culture.
  • 岁终 - Refers to the end of the year, a significant time for settling accounts and reflecting on the past year's activities.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 穆卜之期早逝乎 - Rhetorical question. 'Mù Bū’s time of burial has come early?' or 'Has the time for Mù Bū’s burial arrived too soon?' The phrase uses '期' (time) and '早逝' (early death) in a metaphorical sense, referring to the timing of the funeral.
  • 因无吉地，暂停枢寺院内 - 'Because there is no auspicious place, the coffin is temporarily kept within the temple.' This is a formal and archaic way of expressing the temporary storage of the coffin.
  • 公遂请善风鉴者为之择地 - 'The official then invited someone skilled in geomancy to choose a site.' '善风鉴者' refers to a practitioner of feng shui or geomancy.
  • 为之耗银钱，其事小；为之毕大事，其德巨也 - 'To spend silver and money on this is a small matter; to complete a great matter for him is a great virtue.' This is a contrast between material cost and moral significance.
  • 称贷钱行银若干两 - 'Borrowed money from the moneylender, a certain amount of silver.' '称贷' is a formal term for borrowing money.
  • 债主闻濋移柩葬绵，从之来，坐索不去 - 'The moneylender heard that Chú moved the coffin to be buried in Mian, and came himself, sitting and demanding payment without leaving.' '坐索不去' is an idiom meaning to sit and demand payment without leaving, indicating stubbornness or persistence.
  • 分三次，吾代还之，以数月为限，可否？ - 'In three installments, I will repay on your behalf, with a limit of several months, is this acceptable?' A polite and formal way of requesting a payment plan.
  • 须将借券改立君名下 - 'You must change the loan contract to be in your name.' '改立君名下' is a formal phrase for transferring the liability to another person.
  • 公亲笔立券，债主始去 - 'The official personally wrote the contract, and the moneylender finally left.' '亲笔立券' is a formal expression of personally signing the contract.
  • 公按期如数归还，馀项为之无存 - 'The official repaid the amount on time, and the remaining debt was no longer present.' This is a formal way of saying the debt was fully settled.
  • 濋母子心实有不安焉 - 'Chú and his mother felt truly uneasy about this.' This is a formal and indirect way of expressing inner turmoil or discomfort.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 枢寺院内 - Inside the temple where the coffin is temporarily kept. This refers to a Buddhist or Daoist temple where the coffin is stored before burial, a common practice in traditional Chinese funerary customs.
  • 善风鉴者 - A person skilled in feng shui or geomancy. In traditional Chinese culture, selecting a burial site was considered a critical matter, often requiring the expertise of a geomancer.
  • 称贷 - A formal term for borrowing money, often from a moneylender or bank.
  • 债主 - A moneylender or creditor.
  • 借券 - A loan contract or promissory note. In classical Chinese, such contracts were formal documents.
  • 绵州 - A historical administrative region in Sichuan province. It was an important location during the Qing dynasty.
  • 仁寿 - This may refer to a place, likely a town or district near Mianzhou, where the coffin was moved for burial.
  • 母子 - A term indicating mother and son, common in classical Chinese family structure references.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 熔铅灌死 - This refers to a method of execution or murder where molten lead is poured into a person's mouth or other body parts, causing death. This was a known method of execution in ancient China.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 善定身术 - Literal translation meaning 'good at fixing one's body.' Refers to a magical ability to control one's body.

  • 既能吐人言，必能化人身 - Literal translation meaning 'if you can speak human language, you must be able to transform into a human body.' Implies a magical transformation.
  • 吾愿以汝为妻也 - Literal translation meaning 'I wish to take you as my wife.' Expresses a romantic desire.
  • 微言曰 - Literal translation meaning 'whispered.' '微言' means 'to speak softly' or 'to whisper.'
  • 吾父来矣 - Literal translation meaning 'my father is coming.' A polite way of referring to the male fox's father.
  • 敬祈宽恕 - Literal translation meaning 'please forgive us.' '敬祈' is a polite way of making a request.
  • 解法术 - Literal translation meaning 'to release the magic.' Refers to the man stopping his magical abilities.
  • 果见对面来一老人 - Literal translation meaning 'he indeed saw an old man coming from the opposite direction.' '果' emphasizes the fact that it happened as expected.
  • 宝镜东升 - Literal translation meaning 'the precious mirror rose in the east.' A poetic way of saying 'the sun rose in the east.'
  • 渠宅中有窖藏数千金 - Literal translation meaning 'there are thousands of gold coins hidden in the house.' '渠' is a pronoun meaning 'that' or 'it.'
  • 可急市之 - Literal translation meaning 'you can quickly buy it.' '市' means 'to buy' or 'to trade.'
  • 宅中房舍甚多 - Literal translation meaning 'the house has many rooms.' '甚多' means 'very many.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 窖藏 - Refers to hidden treasures, often associated with wealth and secret wealth in classical Chinese literature.

  • 宅 - Refers to a house or residence, often used to describe the setting of a story.
  • 房舍 - Refers to rooms or buildings, often used to describe the layout of a house.
  • 旷 - Refers to leaving something empty or abandoned, often used to describe a state of neglect.

  • 屠人 - Refers to a butcher, a common occupation in classical Chinese society, often depicted in literature to highlight the harsh realities of life.
